verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1 (Heat Shock Protein 70), a canonical cytosolic/nuclear chaperone. Under baseline conditions it is predominantly intracellular (cytosol, nucleus). However, it is one of the best-documented examples of stress-induced and tumor-cell-surface externalization: heat shock, oxidative stress, hypoxia, and oncogenic transformation drive active translocation of HSP70 to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it is detectable by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells, and anti-HSP70 antibodies on live tumor cells. This surface pool is exploited by NK cell–mediated killing (via NKG2D/NKp46 interaction with surface HSP70) and is the basis for multiple cancer-targeted therapeutic and imaging programs. Immunogenic/programmed cell death also exposes HSP70 on the outer leaflet. The mechanism of externalization is debated (non-classical secretion, exosomal release followed by membrane re-insertion, direct PM translocation), but the outer-leaflet pool is well-documented specifically in cancer and stressed cells. No stable TM domain or GPI anchor; display is cell-state-induced.
